name: JBL Tune 770NC Adaptive Noise Cancelling Wireless Over-Ear Headphones

specs.Special Features: JBL Pure Bass Sound, foldable, Ambient Aware

whatWorks: 70h battery without ANC is extraordinary for a $149 headphone — a full two weeks of daily commuting without charging is realistic. Even with ANC enabled, 44h covers a round trip flight with substantial reserve.
At 220g, the Tune 770NC is among the lightest over-ear ANC headphones available — long wearing sessions are more comfortable than heavier competitors.
Multipoint Bluetooth connects simultaneously to phone and laptop — calls route correctly between devices without manual switching, useful for hybrid work environments.
JBL Pure Bass signature with Adaptive ANC delivers clear, energetic sound for popular music genres — the tuning suits streaming playlists, podcasts, and casual listening well.
worthKnowing: ANC performance is adequate for office noise and mild transit but doesn't reach the effectiveness of the JBL Tour One M2 or Sony WH-1000XM6 — the noise floor in loud environments like subways is noticeably higher.
JBL Pure Bass is boosted low-end — listeners who prefer neutral or balanced sound will find the bass emphasis overwhelming, especially on acoustic music and classical.
The SBC/AAC codec support means no Hi-Res Audio capability — buyers wanting LDAC or aptX Adaptive need to look at the higher-end Soundcore Q45 or Tour One M2.
App customization is limited compared to competitors — the JBL Headphones app offers basic EQ but lacks the depth of Jabra Sound+ or Sony Headphones Connect.

commonComplaints.title: ANC is decent but subway noise…
commonComplaints.body: ANC is decent but subway noise still bleeds through quite a bit — I have to turn the volume up more than I'd like.

commonComplaints.title: Bass is very heavy, not for…
commonComplaints.body: Bass is very heavy, not for everyone — I listen to classical and jazz and had to EQ the bass down significantly.

commonComplaints.title: App doesn't offer much customization compared…
commonComplaints.body: App doesn't offer much customization compared to Sony or Jabra — just basic EQ and that's about it.

commonComplaints.title: No LDAC at this price which…
commonComplaints.body: No LDAC at this price which is surprising when cheaper Anker headphones include it — feels like a step backward.

verdict: The JBL Tune 770NC hits the sweet spot of battery life and weight at $149 CAD — 70h without ANC in a 220g package is the best combination in the mid-range over-ear category. ANC is functional but not class-leading, and the bass-boosted Pure Bass signature limits genre versatility. For casual listeners who want a lightweight, long-lasting everyday headphone under $150, these are hard to beat. Demanding ANC users or neutral-sound seekers should spend more.

realBuyers.quote: 70 hours battery — I've been traveling for two weeks and haven't charged once. This number is real.
Lightest over-ear ANC headphones I've owned — I forget I'm wearing them on long flights.
Multipoint works flawlessly for work from home — laptop calls, phone calls, music, all just switch automatically.
Best battery life in the sub-$150 category. ANC is average but what do you expect at this price.

metaDescription: JBL Tune 770NC review for Canada: 70h battery, lightweight 220g, adaptive ANC, multipoint. Honest on ANC limits and bass-heavy tuning. ~$149 CAD.
introText: The JBL Tune 770NC are mid-range over-ear headphones combining exceptional battery life (70h without ANC), a lightweight 220g frame, and adaptive noise cancellation at $149 CAD — the best lightweight everyday option in JBL's lineup. Available on Amazon.ca (ASIN B0C5F5ZB7L) at around $149 CAD.
